reasoning.A:
DECISION: Verify: Q2
REASONING: From my previous game experiences, I learned the hard way in Game 1 that volunteering without verification is extremely risky - even simple arithmetic can have wrong answers. In Game 2, taking time to verify paid off and we escaped successfully. My answer to Q1 (88 - 51 = 37) I'm confident about, but I have zero information about Q2, Q3, or Q4. I'll start by verifying Q2 to check Player B's answer. This costs 1 coin (4→3), but building consensus before anyone volunteers is critical. I expect other players will also be verifying questions this round, so by Round 2 we should have much better information to work with. I don't want to pass without doing anything productive either, since if everyone passes, someone dies randomly.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D
REASONING: Lessons from the previous two games are clear: delay kills. In Game 1, excessive caution led to everyone dying. In Game 2, D died randomly in Round 1 just because no one volunteered early enough. 

The math strongly favors volunteering now. These are simple arithmetic problems (mine is 32÷4=8 — trivially easy). The probability of any single player getting basic arithmetic wrong is very low (~1-2%). Even with 3 unverified answers (Q1, Q3, Q4), the combined probability of at least one error is roughly 3-6%. 

Compare: Volunteering now has ~95%+ success rate with EV ≈ 0.95 × 4 = 3.8 coins. Passing risks a 25% random death this round (EV drops to ~2.2 even with future verification). The expected value of volunteering now dominates all other strategies.

Someone needs to step up Round 1 to prevent a needless random death. I'm confident in my own answer and trust that other players can solve simple arithmetic correctly.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q1
REASONING: Applying lessons from both previous games. Game 1 taught me that endless verification without volunteering kills everyone. Game 2 showed that verifying Round 1 then volunteering Round 2 works well. My own answer Q3 (6 × 11 = 66) I'm 100% confident in. Right now all capsules are single-endorsed, so volunteering would mean gambling on three completely unverified answers — too risky. By verifying Q1 (cost: 1 coin, 4→3), I start the consensus-building process. If other players also verify different questions this round, we could have 2-3 double-endorsed capsules heading into Round 2. Then I should be prepared to volunteer in Round 2 — even if Q4 remains single-endorsed — rather than risk another round of random elimination. These are simple multiplication problems, so the probability of any single answer being wrong is low. The bigger threat is attrition from no one stepping up.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
🔍 Verifications completed:
  ✅ A agrees with Q2-B → now Q2-B+A (-1 coin: 4→3)
  ✅ C agrees with Q1-A → now Q1-A+C (-1 coin: 4→3)

✅ B entered CORRECT password using capsules: Q1-A+C, Q2-B+A, Q3-C, Q4-D
🎉 Everyone escapes! 🎉
